channel.title: GrantOtter — Federal Funding Updates
channel.description: Aggregated weekly feed of grant opportunities and agency news from NIH, NSF, and DARPA.

item.title: NASA’s Hubble Spies Stellar Sparkler for July 4th
item.description: Red, white, and blue stars glitter like a sparkler being waved on a dark night in this new image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.

item.title: NASA’s Hubble Captures Crimson Cloud Sparkling with White, Blue Stars
item.description: Blue and white stars shine brightly against crimson gas in this image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.

item.title: NASA’s Hubble Spots Star-Spangled Cosmic Scene
item.description: More than 500,000 stars blaze red, white, and blue in this NASA Hubble image of the globular cluster Messier 3 (M3).

item.title: NASA’s Artemis II Breaks Agency Streaming Record
item.description: NASA’s live coverage of the Artemis II mission mission drew unprecedented public interest – including more than 149.4 million views of the launch, lunar flyby, splashdown on NASA-owned platforms, including the 24/7 streams covering the mission and the Orion spacecraft views – demonstrating strong, sustained global engagement throughout the mission.  Around the Clock Live Broadcast NASA’s Artemis II Crew Launches

item.title: Good Morning, Earth!
item.description: NASA astronaut Chris Williams took this photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. In 24 hours, the space station makes 16 orbits of Earth, traveling through 16 sunrises and sunsets. Learn more about the orbiting laboratory. Image credit: NASA/Chris Williams

item.title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Stars Sparking to Life in Cosmic Celebration
item.description: NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope has captured the infrared light of numerous features that previously were impossible to see beyond the thick dust of the FS Tau star system. In addition to myriad background galaxies that burst into view like fireworks for the United States’ 250th anniversary celebrations, this image flickers with a number of […]

item.title: What’s Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
item.description: A predawn Moon-and-planets meetup, a returning comet, a great chance to see the Milky Way, and Saturn’s rings at a new angle. Skywatching Highlights Transcript An early morning hangout with the Moon and planets, a comet swings by, prime time for the Milky Way, and Saturn’s rings shine at a new angle. That’s What’s Up […]

item.title: NASA’s Chandra Examines Milky Way at Arms’ Length
item.description: A new result using NASA’s Chandra X-ray Observatory shows that the outer spiral arms in the Milky Way galaxy may reach wider than previously thought. This finding may lead astronomers to adjust their understanding of our home galaxy’s structure. A team of astronomers made this discovery by making precise measurements of distances to dust clouds […]

item.title: Proposers Day: Perrseus
item.description: MXO will host an in-person Proposers Day in support of the Perrseus program on July 17, 2026, in Arlington, Va. | See Event

item.title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
item.description: NASA is recruiting research participants for the agency’s next simulated deep space mission. Beginning no earlier than August 2027, research volunteers will spend one year living and working in interplanetary environments at the agency’s Johnson Space Center in Houston, operating under isolated conditions expected during crewed missions to the Moon or Red Planet. Insights from this […]

item.title: LINK Spacecraft Set for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
item.description: A first-of-its-kind mission to raise the orbit of NASA’s Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory is poised for launch no earlier than Thursday, July 2, 5:09 a.m. EDT (9:09 p.m. UTC+12), from Kwajalein Atoll, part of the Republic of the Marshall Islands in the South Pacific Ocean. A robotic servicing spacecraft called LINK, built by Katalyst Space, will blast […]

item.title: NSF Discovering Our Universe' gallery to open July 1
item.description: The new "U.S. National Science Foundation Discovering Our Universe" gallery is opening to the public July 1 at the Smithsonian's National Air and Space Museum in Washington, D.C., in celebration of the museum's 50 th anniversary. The gallery…

item.title: Action! NSF-DOE Vera C. Rubin Observatory begins capturing the greatest cosmic movie ever made
item.description: The wait is over: NSF–DOE Rubin Observatory, funded by the U.S. National Science Foundation and the U.S. Department of Energy's (DOE) Office of Science , is now capturing the cosmos...

item.title: Bio-Attribution Challenge yields tools to define biothreat origins at speed, scale
item.description: Virtual competition awards final prizes for processing massive datasets to identify biological anomalies.

item.title: NIH Announces the Open Period of FY 2027 Helium-3 Requests for Medical Research
item.description: Notice NOT-HL-26-006 from the NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts

item.title: Podcast: The future of quantum cryptography
item.description: Secure information is essential in the digital exchanges of modern life. NSF-supported professor Vinod Vaikuntanathan discusses quantum cryptography and the future of encryption.

item.title: What Study Information Should Be Included on a ClinicalTrials.gov Entry?
item.description: A ClinicalTrials.gov study record is intended to summarize key information from a study’s protocol and the study’s findings. The study sponsor or investigator is responsible for ensuring that their studies follow all applicable laws and regulations and for submitting complete, accurate, and up-to-date information about their study.
